---
name: "\U0001F331 Feature Request — Make ICE Websites Easier to Use"
about: Suggest a feature that would help families, friends, or advocates better use
  ICE websites to find and support detained loved ones.
title: 'Problem using ICE website: [brief description]'
labels: feature-request
assignees: Redwood74

---

**You do NOT need to know how to code to submit this.**  
If something about an ICE website confused you, slowed you down, or made it harder to find or help someone in detention — you are in the right place.

You can write as little or as much as you want.

---

## Start here (quick version)

**In one or two sentences, what problem did you run into?**

> _Example: “I couldn’t tell which detention facility my brother was moved to after last week.”_

---

## If you have the energy, please tell us more  
*(Optional, but very helpful)*

### 1. What were you trying to do?
> _For example: find someone in ICE custody, confirm their location, understand their case status, check transfer history, etc._

---

### 2. What part of the ICE website was involved?
> _If you know the page name or link, great. If not, just describe what you remember._

---

### 3. What made this hard, confusing, or stressful?
> _Unclear language, missing information, errors, slow updates, accessibility barriers, unexpected logouts, etc._

---

### 4. What do you wish the website (or a tool like ICEpicks) could do instead?
> _You don’t need to design a solution — just describe what would have helped you._

> _Example: “A clear timeline showing when someone was transferred and where they went.”_

---

### 5. Who would this help?
_Check all that apply:_
- [ ] Family members  
- [ ] Friends  
- [ ] Attorneys  
- [ ] Advocates / NGOs  
- [ ] People currently in ICE custody  
- [ ] Other (please describe)

---

## Optional context (only share what feels safe)

- General location (city/state or country):  
- Language(s) you prefer to use:  
- Accessibility needs (mobile-only, screen reader, low vision, limited bandwidth, etc.):

---

## ❤️ A note to everyone reading this

Many feature requests here come from people dealing with urgency, fear, and real harm.  
There is no such thing as a “bad” feature request.

If you are a developer or contributor:
- Assume good faith  
- Ask follow‑up questions with care  
- Design for dignity
